discussion_posts.name: shrekk1555
discussion_posts.content:
Hi All,
In this problem, as per my understanding the output should be 1 and 2 as the business_id, isnt it ?
1 for "reviews" (7+3/2 = 5) < 7 and ads (11+7+6/3=8) < 11
2 for page views (3+12/2=7.5) < 12

Please correct if my understanding is wrong.

discussion_posts.name: docxeg249
discussion_posts.content: Why is everyone posting the solutions in the discussion section? There is a seperate section for that !

discussion_posts.name: Bang25
discussion_posts.content:
/* Write your PL/SQL query statement below */


with subq as
(
select event_type, avg(occurences) as average
    from events
    group by event_type)

select events.business_id as business_id
from events join subq on events.event_type = subq.event_type
and events.occurences > subq.average
group by events.business_id having count(events.business_id)>1;;

discussion_posts.name: caelandavey
discussion_posts.content: In the Pandas Schema occurrences is spelt incorrectly.

discussion_posts.name: Chaochao1990
discussion_posts.content:
with t as (select event_type, avg(occurences) o_avg from Events group by 1)
select business_id
from Events join t using(event_type)
where occurences>o_avg
group by 1
having count(1)>1

discussion_posts.name: Jeffrey_Zhao
discussion_posts.content:
with a as(select business_id,event_type, occurences, avg(occurences) over (partition by event_type) as ave
from events
)

select distinct business_id
from a
where occurences>ave
group by business_id
having count(distinct event_type)>1

discussion_posts.name: mikey121
discussion_posts.content:
'''
SELECT business_id 

FROM EVENTS, (select event_type , avg(occurences) av from events  group by event_type) a 

where Events.event_type = a.event_type and Events.occurences > a.av 

group by business_id

having count(Events.occurences > a.av ) > 1

'''

discussion_posts.name: santa_clara_zhazha
yz541
discussion_posts.content:
with CTE as (
select business_id, event_type, occurences, avg(occurences)over(partition by event_type) as AVG_OCC from Events)

select business_id FROM CTE where occurences > AVG_OCC group by business_id HAVING COUNT(event_type) > 1

discussion_posts.name: aswathr
discussion_posts.content:
''' 
select 
e.business_id
from Events e 
join 
(
select event_type, avg(occurences) as event_avg
from Events
group by event_type 
)a  on e.event_type= a.event_type 
and e.occurences > a. event_avg  
group by e.business_id
having count(distinct e.event_type)>1 
'''
